title: Thick target neutron yields from Beryllium, Carbon, Tungsten, and Lead targets irradiated by 26.7 MeV/nucleon 4He ions

x4freetext: The Radioactive Ion Beam Line in
Lanzhou (RIBLL)
Neutrons were distinguished from photons by
using the pulse shape discrimination method.
Counts of incident beam particles were
continuously monitored by a 0.5 mm thick plastic
scintillator detector.
Three BC501A organic liquid scintillation
detectors were employed to measure the secondary
neutrons at 0 deg, 37 deg, and 60 deg with respect to
the beam direction in the laboratory frame. All the
BC501A detectors have identical cylindrical shape
with areal size of 12.7 cm diameter and 12.7 cm long.
Total uncertainty
The statistical errors were less
than 5% at energies below 20 MeV and increased to 35%
at high-energy end from the neutron counts.
The uncertainty of the detection efficiency
is estimated to be less than 10% for the incident
neutron-energy range of 0.1-80 MeV, and slightly
exceeds 10% with higher energy neutrons.
The overall uncertainty of the neutron
detection to 15% in the analysis.
